ops.title: golangci phantom findings also happen LOCALLY when agent worktrees are deleted — findings cite dead .claude/worktrees/ paths; cache clean, never code fixes
ops.body:
The golangci stale-cache phantom pattern (see [[golangci-actions-cache-phantom-findings]]) also fires **locally**: after `.claude/worktrees/*` agent worktrees are removed, `golangci-lint run` in the main checkout can report findings whose file paths point INTO the deleted worktree (`.claude/worktrees/<name>/internal/...`), alongside "can't read file / no such file or directory" processor warnings.

**Why:** golangci's analysis cache indexes by package; concurrently-linted worktree copies poison it, and after removal the cached issues resurface against dead paths.

**How to apply:** a finding whose path contains `.claude/worktrees/` is never real — run `golangci-lint cache clean && golangci-lint run` (expect 0 issues), never "fix" code for it. Seen twice on 2026-07-11 during the v0.24.0 DRY-up (1 gosec, then 7 gosec, both vanished on clean cache).
